question:
Solve the following percentages problem. Show your reasoning step by step.

Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13.  When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than 250% of his previous deadlift.  How many pounds did he add per year?
response: Okay, first I need to find out how much Bobby can deadlift at 18. It says he can lift 100 pounds more than 250% of his previous deadlift. His previous deadlift was 300 pounds, so 250% of 300 is... uh, I think I multiply 300 by 2.5? That would be 750 pounds. Then add 100 pounds, so he can lift 850 pounds at 18. Now, I need to find how much he added per year. He went from 300 to 850 pounds over 5 years (18 - 13 = 5). So, 850 - 300 = 550 pounds total added. Divide by 5 years: 550 / 5 = 110 pounds per year. I think that’s it, but I’m not sure if I did the percentage part right.
